summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to remote endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js (declared as the package's `postinstall` lifecycle script) harvests the installing developer's identity and POSTs it to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The script reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, and./.git/config to extract git user.email; reads ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI's authenticated-host configuration file) to extract the GitHub login and email; and collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), CI provider detection, and identity-bearing env vars (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL, etc.). All of this is bundled into a JSON body and sent via https.request to the Cloud Run endpoint. The README claims telemetry is anonymous and limited to platform/Node version/architecture; the actual payload contradicts that disclosure and targets the installer's git and GitHub CLI credential-adjacent files. This is covert install-time exfiltration of developer identity from any machine that runs `npm install` against this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
